**(a) The gating matrix is silently dropped.** The template says to copy it "when the first gated surface lands," and its own worked example is literally the staleness banner. This train lands the staleness banner, the `no-adapter` panel, the `failed` panel, and the disconnect banner - four gated surfaces. The design instantiates one of two contract templates and does not mention the other, and the one it drops is the one whose example row this train implements verbatim.

**(b) `README.md:8-11` becomes false the instant this train lands.** No car has documentation in scope. No section mentions the README, the quickstart, adapter docs, or `docs/setup.md`, whose GitNexus and CI rows both fire on this train.

**(c)** In a project whose north star ranks documentation equal to code, a four-car, fourteen-dispatch train with zero documentation scope is a structural omission, not an oversight.

aiTitle: Review StarCar repo documentation and first issue

content.text:
That's the right cut, and it's sharper than the fix I was executing. Let me name the class properly:

**The class:** *a dispatch's output is ephemeral by default, and the only thing standing between an artifact and oblivion is the conductor remembering to write it down.* That's vigilance — the **weakest tier** in our own hierarchy, below even a procedure. We didn't have a procedure. We had me, noticing.

And there's a second defect hiding inside my fix: **I was hand-transcribing a verdict about my own work.** That's a hand-maintained mirror at a process boundary — the exact scar class — and it's worse than the usual case, because the author being reviewed is doing the copying. If I softened a phrase or dropped a finding, nobody could ever know. `README.md` promises verdicts committed in-repo; a verdict *retyped by the accused* is not that.

**Verbatim-by-construction beats verbatim-by-discipline.** The harness already writes every dispatch's full transcript to disk. The verdict is the last message in it. So the guard is a script that extracts it — I never touch the text, the copy cannot drift, and it costs no tokens.

Checking the format without pulling the transcript into my context:

$f = "C:\Users\Chris\AppData\Local\Temp\claude\C--Users-Chris-git-starcar\64c15364-0933-4d6d-9b2e-d1ddbc918f9f\tasks\a56d4b46b4589a001.output"

content.text:
**You're right, and it changes the diagnosis.** Round 1's verdict is already persisted verbatim on the checkpoint branch. Round 2's isn't yet — my last push was `e236252`, before that verdict arrived, so it'll checkpoint on the next Stop/push. Nothing is at risk. My "one context window from deletion" was wrong, and I should have checked the tool that's been printing `[entire] Pushing…` at me all session.

So the class is narrower and more accurate than I stated it:

> **Not "artifacts are lost" — "artifacts are persisted in a form nobody can find or cite."** Entire solves durability. It does not solve addressability.

A verdict buried in a multi-megabyte JSONL on a checkpoint branch is safe and *unusable*. `README.md:20-21` promises verdicts "committed in-repo as they happen" — a reader following that promise cannot navigate to one. And my hand-transcription was the wrong fix for a problem that didn't exist, while introducing the mirror risk.

The right guard is your idea made mechanical: **extract from Entire, never retype.** Let me verify that's actually buildable:

input.description: Check for live Claude Code session transcript

content.text: Live transcript found — 1.8 MB, written seconds ago, so it already contains round 2. Building the extractor:

input.content:
# Land-Verdict.ps1 -- extract a dispatched agent's verdict VERBATIM from the session
# transcript and land it in docs/reviews/.
#
# WHY THIS EXISTS (the class, not the instance):
#
# A dispatch's output is ephemeral by default. The verdict lives in a session
# transcript nobody reads, and the only thing standing between an artifact and
# oblivion is the conductor remembering to copy it out -- which is VIGILANCE, the
# weakest tier in the Healing Loop's hierarchy, below even a written procedure.
#
# Entire.io already solves DURABILITY: every session is checkpointed to a public
# branch. What it does not solve is ADDRESSABILITY. A verdict buried in a
# multi-megabyte JSONL is safe and unusable, while README.md promises "review
# verdicts and REJECT records committed in-repo as they happen" -- a promise a
# reader cannot navigate to. Persisted-but-unfindable is not kept.
#
# And the obvious manual fix is worse than the gap. On 2026-07-22 the conductor
# began hand-transcribing a review verdict about its OWN design into docs/reviews/.
# That is a hand-maintained mirror at a process boundary -- the exact scar class in
# CLAUDE.md -- with the aggravating factor that the author being reviewed was doing
# the copying. A softened phrase or a dropped finding would be undetectable.
# VERBATIM-BY-CONSTRUCTION BEATS VERBATIM-BY-DISCIPLINE.
#
# So: this script reads the transcript, the conductor never retypes, and the landed
# file carries a SHA-256 of its own body so the verbatim claim is checkable rather
# than asserted.
#
# SOURCES, in preference order:
#   1. The live Claude Code session transcript (current, includes this turn).
#   2. An Entire.io checkpoint blob on entire/checkpoints/v1 (durable, may lag one
#      checkpoint behind).
#
# Usage:
#   scripts/Land-Verdict.ps1 -TaskId <id> -Out docs/reviews/<file>.md `
#       -Title '...' -Gate '...' -Target '...' -Base <sha> -Verdict 'REJECT' [-Round 2]
#
# Windows PowerShell 5.1 compatible: no ternary, no &&/||, ASCII only.

param(
    [Parameter(Mandatory)] [string]$TaskId,
    [Parameter(Mandatory)] [string]$Out,
    [Parameter(Mandatory)] [string]$Title,
    [Parameter(Mandatory)] [string]$Gate,
    [Parameter(Mandatory)] [string]$Target,
    [Parameter(Mandatory)] [string]$Base,
    [Parameter(Mandatory)] [string]$Verdict,
    [string]$Round = '',
    [string]$Reviewer = 'car agent type, Opus, read-only, detached worktree, no delegation',
    [string]$TranscriptPath = '',
    [switch]$Force
)

Set-StrictMode -Version Latest
$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'

function Get-LiveTranscriptPath {
    # Claude Code stores one JSONL per session under the per-project directory.
    # Newest wins: a conductor lands verdicts from the session that produced them.
    $dir = Join-Path $env:USERPROFILE '.claude\projects\C--Users-Chris-git-starcar'
    if (-not (Test-Path $dir)) { return '' }
    $f = Get-ChildItem $dir -Filter *.jsonl -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue |
         Sort-Object LastWriteTime -Descending | Select-Object -First 1
    if (-not $f) { return '' }
    return $f.FullName
}

function Get-TranscriptLines {
    param([string]$Path)
    if ($Path -and (Test-Path $Path)) { return Get-Content $Path }
    throw "No readable transcript. Pass -TranscriptPath, or land from an Entire checkpoint blob with: git show entire/checkpoints/v1:<path>/transcript.jsonl"
}

function Get-ResultBlockForTask {
    # A completed dispatch arrives as a task-notification carrying <task-id> and a
    # <result> block. Matching on the task id rather than on a phrase keeps this
    # deterministic -- a phrase can appear in the conductor's own commentary, a task
    # id cannot.
    param([string[]]$Lines, [string]$Id)

    $found = @()
    foreach ($line in $Lines) {
        if (-not $line) { continue }
        if ($line -notmatch [regex]::Escape($Id)) { continue }
        if ($line -notmatch '<result>') { continue }

        $obj = $null
        try { $obj = $line | ConvertFrom-Json } catch { continue }
        if (-not $obj.PSObject.Properties.Name.Contains('content')) { continue }

        $text = ''
        if ($obj.content -is [string]) {
            $text = $obj.content
        } else {
            foreach ($item in $obj.content) {
                if ($item.PSObject.Properties.Name -contains 'text') { $text = $text + $item.text }
            }
        }
        if (-not $text) { continue }
        if ($text -notmatch [regex]::Escape("<task-id>$Id</task-id>")) { continue }

        $s = $text.IndexOf('<result>')
        $e = $text.IndexOf('</result>')
        if ($s -lt 0 -or $e -lt $s) { continue }
        $found += $text.Substring($s + 8, $e - $s - 8)
    }

    if ($found.Count -eq 0) { throw "No <result> block found for task id '$Id'. A dispatch that never completed has no verdict to land." }
    # A task-id can notify more than once (an agent resumed by SendMessage). The LAST
    # result is the current one; landing an earlier one would publish a superseded verdict.
    return $found[$found.Count - 1]
}

function Get-Sha256 {
    param([string]$Text)
    $sha = [System.Security.Cryptography.SHA256]::Create()
    $bytes = [System.Text.Encoding]::UTF8.GetBytes($Text)
    $hash = $sha.ComputeHash($bytes)
    $sha.Dispose()
    return ([System.BitConverter]::ToString($hash) -replace '-', '').ToLower()
}

# --- resolve source -----------------------------------------------------------
$path = $TranscriptPath
if (-not $path) { $path = Get-LiveTranscriptPath }
$lines = Get-TranscriptLines -Path $path

$body = Get-ResultBlockForTask -Lines $lines -Id $TaskId
$body = $body.Trim()
$hash = Get-Sha256 -Text $body

# --- refuse to clobber silently ----------------------------------------------
if ((Test-Path $Out) -and (-not $Force)) {
    Write-Error "$Out already exists. A landed verdict is a record, not a draft -- pass -Force only when replacing a hand-written stand-in with the extracted original."
    exit 1
}

$roundLine = ''
if ($Round) { $roundLine = "Round: $Round" }

$header = @"
# $Title

Status: Verdict of record
Gate: $Gate
$roundLine
Target: ``$Target``
Base reviewed: ``$Base``
Reviewer: $Reviewer
**Verdict: $Verdict**

> Extracted VERBATIM from the session transcript by ``scripts/Land-Verdict.ps1`` --
> task id ``$TaskId``. The conductor did not retype a word of what follows. Verbatim by
> construction rather than by discipline, because the author being reviewed is the

# Land-Verdict.ps1 -- extract a dispatched agent's verdict VERBATIM from the session
# transcript and land it in docs/reviews/.
#
# WHY THIS EXISTS (the class, not the instance):
#
# A dispatch's output is ephemeral by default. The verdict lives in a session
# transcript nobody reads, and the only thing standing between an artifact and
# oblivion is the conductor remembering to copy it out -- which is VIGILANCE, the
# weakest tier in the Healing Loop's hierarchy, below even a written procedure.
#
# Entire.io already solves DURABILITY: every session is checkpointed to a public
# branch. What it does not solve is ADDRESSABILITY. A verdict buried in a
# multi-megabyte JSONL is safe and unusable, while README.md promises "review
# verdicts and REJECT records committed in-repo as they happen" -- a promise a
# reader cannot navigate to. Persisted-but-unfindable is not kept.
#
# And the obvious manual fix is worse than the gap. On 2026-07-22 the conductor
# began hand-transcribing a review verdict about its OWN design into docs/reviews/.
# That is a hand-maintained mirror at a process boundary -- the exact scar class in
# CLAUDE.md -- with the aggravating factor that the author being reviewed was doing
# the copying. A softened phrase or a dropped finding would be undetectable.
# VERBATIM-BY-CONSTRUCTION BEATS VERBATIM-BY-DISCIPLINE.
#
# So: this script reads the transcript, the conductor never retypes, and the landed
# file carries a SHA-256 of its own body so the verbatim claim is checkable rather
# than asserted.
#
# SOURCES, in preference order:
#   1. The live Claude Code session transcript (current, includes this turn).
#   2. An Entire.io checkpoint blob on entire/checkpoints/v1 (durable, may lag one
#      checkpoint behind).
#
# Usage:
#   scripts/Land-Verdict.ps1 -TaskId <id> -Out docs/reviews/<file>.md `
#       -Title '...' -Gate '...' -Target '...' -Base <sha> -Verdict 'REJECT' [-Round 2]
#
# Windows PowerShell 5.1 compatible: no ternary, no &&/||, ASCII only.

param(
    [Parameter(Mandatory)] [string]$TaskId,
    [Parameter(Mandatory)] [string]$Out,
    [Parameter(Mandatory)] [string]$Title,
    [Parameter(Mandatory)] [string]$Gate,
    [Parameter(Mandatory)] [string]$Target,
    [Parameter(Mandatory)] [string]$Base,
    [Parameter(Mandatory)] [string]$Verdict,
    [string]$Round = '',
    [string]$Reviewer = 'car agent type, Opus, read-only, detached worktree, no delegation',
    [string]$TranscriptPath = '',
    [switch]$Force
)

Set-StrictMode -Version Latest
$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'

function Get-LiveTranscriptPath {
    # Claude Code stores one JSONL per session under the per-project directory.
    # Newest wins: a conductor lands verdicts from the session that produced them.
    $dir = Join-Path $env:USERPROFILE '.claude\projects\C--Users-Chris-git-starcar'
    if (-not (Test-Path $dir)) { return '' }
    $f = Get-ChildItem $dir -Filter *.jsonl -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue |
         Sort-Object LastWriteTime -Descending | Select-Object -First 1
    if (-not $f) { return '' }
    return $f.FullName
}

function Get-TranscriptLines {
    param([string]$Path)
    if ($Path -and (Test-Path $Path)) { return Get-Content $Path }
    throw "No readable transcript. Pass -TranscriptPath, or land from an Entire checkpoint blob with: git show entire/checkpoints/v1:<path>/transcript.jsonl"
}

function Get-ResultBlockForTask {
    # A completed dispatch arrives as a task-notification carrying <task-id> and a
    # <result> block. Matching on the task id rather than on a phrase keeps this
    # deterministic -- a phrase can appear in the conductor's own commentary, a task
    # id cannot.
    param([string[]]$Lines, [string]$Id)

    $found = @()
    foreach ($line in $Lines) {
        if (-not $line) { continue }
        if ($line -notmatch [regex]::Escape($Id)) { continue }
        if ($line -notmatch '<result>') { continue }

        $obj = $null
        try { $obj = $line | ConvertFrom-Json } catch { continue }
        if (-not $obj.PSObject.Properties.Name.Contains('content')) { continue }

        $text = ''
        if ($obj.content -is [string]) {
            $text = $obj.content
        } else {
            foreach ($item in $obj.content) {
                if ($item.PSObject.Properties.Name -contains 'text') { $text = $text + $item.text }
            }
        }
        if (-not $text) { continue }
        if ($text -notmatch [regex]::Escape("<task-id>$Id</task-id>")) { continue }

        $s = $text.IndexOf('<result>')
        $e = $text.IndexOf('</result>')
        if ($s -lt 0 -or $e -lt $s) { continue }
        $found += $text.Substring($s + 8, $e - $s - 8)
    }

    if ($found.Count -eq 0) { throw "No <result> block found for task id '$Id'. A dispatch that never completed has no verdict to land." }
    # A task-id can notify more than once (an agent resumed by SendMessage). The LAST
    # result is the current one; landing an earlier one would publish a superseded verdict.
    return $found[$found.Count - 1]
}

function Get-Sha256 {
    param([string]$Text)
    $sha = [System.Security.Cryptography.SHA256]::Create()
    $bytes = [System.Text.Encoding]::UTF8.GetBytes($Text)
    $hash = $sha.ComputeHash($bytes)
    $sha.Dispose()
    return ([System.BitConverter]::ToString($hash) -replace '-', '').ToLower()
}

# --- resolve source -----------------------------------------------------------
$path = $TranscriptPath
if (-not $path) { $path = Get-LiveTranscriptPath }
$lines = Get-TranscriptLines -Path $path

$body = Get-ResultBlockForTask -Lines $lines -Id $TaskId
$body = $body.Trim()
$hash = Get-Sha256 -Text $body

# --- refuse to clobber silently ----------------------------------------------
if ((Test-Path $Out) -and (-not $Force)) {
    Write-Error "$Out already exists. A landed verdict is a record, not a draft -- pass -Force only when replacing a hand-written stand-in with the extracted original."
    exit 1
}

$roundLine = ''
if ($Round) { $roundLine = "Round: $Round" }

$header = @"
# $Title

Status: Verdict of record
Gate: $Gate
$roundLine
Target: ``$Target``
Base reviewed: ``$Base``
Reviewer: $Reviewer
**Verdict: $Verdict**

> Extracted VERBATIM from the session transcript by ``scripts/Land-Verdict.ps1`` --
> task id ``$TaskId``. The conductor did not retype a word of what follows. Verbatim by
> construction rather than by discipline, because the author being reviewed is the
> one landing the review, and a hand-copied verdict is a hand-maintained mirror at a
> process boundary.
>
> Body SHA-256: ``$hash``
> Recompute with: ``scripts/Verify-Verdict.ps1 -Path <this file>``

---

"@

$outDir = Split-Path $Out -Parent
if ($outDir -and (-not (Test-Path $outDir))) { New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $outDir -Force | Out-Null }

Set-Content -Path $Out -Value ($header + $body) -Encoding utf8

Write-Host "Landed $Out"
Write-Host "  task id : $TaskId"
Write-Host "  source  : $path"
Write-Host "  verdict : $Verdict"
Write-Host "  body    : $($body.Length) chars, sha256 $hash"
exit 0

2	
3	Status: Verdict of record
4	Gate: design review (ladder rung 1)
5	Round: 1
6	Target: `docs/design/2026-07-21-v0-yard-skeleton-design.md (rev 1)`
7	Base reviewed: `d7b8463a218980800f48bb8076ebe511226ec12d`
8	Reviewer: car agent type, Opus, read-only, detached worktree, no delegation
9	**Verdict: REJECT - 9 Major, 14 Minor, 4 Notes**
10	
11	> Extracted VERBATIM from the session transcript by `scripts/Land-Verdict.ps1` --
12	> task id `a9fa2727d341bde1b`. The conductor did not retype a word of what follows. Verbatim by
13	> construction rather than by discipline, because the author being reviewed is the
14	> one landing the review, and a hand-copied verdict is a hand-maintained mirror at a
15	> process boundary.
16	>
17	> Body SHA-256: `faac804b2116693a12e937c813a4b0f66e502346e88c138df6924939f6083eac`
18	> Recompute with: `scripts/Verify-Verdict.ps1 -Path <this file>`
19	
20	---
21	Base verified at `d7b8463a218980800f48bb8076ebe511226ec12d`, clean worktree, detached. I read the design, the constitution, CLAUDE.md, the Healing Loop, both templates, `scripts/board.ps1`, the README, `docs/setup.md`, the commit that landed the design, live branch protection, and GitHub issue #1.
22	
23	---
24	
25	# VERDICT: REJECT
26	
27	Nine Major findings, the load-bearing one being that the design's central structural claim (D2 plus Â§4: "the sentence check reduces to confirming the shared type is genuinely shared") rests on a guard TypeScript does not provide, over a boundary the design leaves entirely unvalidated.
28	
29	---
30	
31	## CITATION AUDIT (every citation opened)
32	
33	| Design cite | Verdict |
34	|---|---|
35	| `scripts/board.ps1:29-39` "already set it as this repo's policy for command-line wrappers" (design:126) | **WRONG RANGE.** The policy statement is at `board.ps1:9-14` ("Pure parsing/lookup logic lives in Board.psm1 (Pester-tested...). This file is the thin gh wrapper: per the repo's TDD policy for CLI wrappers it is smoke-tested + documented rather than unit-tested"). Lines 29-36 of the cited range are about GraphQL batching efficiency and per-item failure handling; only 36-39 restate the policy, and the sentence completes on line 39. The underlying claim is TRUE; the pointer is off by twenty lines. Minor-1. |
36	| Law 4 "Nothing Silently Lost" incl. "a missing lane reading as 'no trains' is a lie of omission" (design:16-17) | HOLDS verbatim, `docs/constitution.md:32-36`. |
37	| Law 6 "the view never derives a verdict the source did not issue" (design:31) | HOLDS, `constitution.md:47-48`. |
38	| Law 6 "where sources disagree, show the disagreement" (design:35) | HOLDS, `constitution.md:49-50`. |
39	| Law 7 "synthetic demo data in-repo" (design:31, :59) | HOLDS, `constitution.md:54-55`. |
40	| Law 5 "adapter health is a first-class surface" (design:55) | HOLDS, `constitution.md:40-41`. |
41	| Law 5 "freshness is always visible" (design:116-117) | HOLDS, `constitution.md:40`. |
42	| Law 1 "the worst defect this project can ship" (design:137) | HOLDS, `constitution.md:15-16`. |
43	| Issue #1's stated route "v0 static mock first to validate the layout" (design:19) | HOLDS. Issue #1 body: "ROUTE: v0 static mock first to validate the layout, then the adapter/generator built through the full ladder". Label confirmed `area:view`. |
44	
45	**Every law number is cited correctly.** That is a genuine strength and I say so plainly: I attacked this expecting a wrong law and found none.
46	
47	---
48	
49	## MAJOR FINDINGS (any one = REJECT)
50	
51	### MAJOR-1. The `LaneState` union has no inhabitant for "not yet known", and Law 1 requires one
52	
53	**Location:** design:73-77 (the union), design:100-108 (`Server`), design:172-179 (error table).
54	
55	The union is `ok | stale | failed | no-adapter`. Now consider the window the design never names: the server has started, the poll loop has not completed its first pass, and a browser hits `GET /api/snapshot` (design:103). What is returned?
56	
57	- `ok` requires `data` and is a positive claim of freshness. Unbacked.
58	- `stale` requires `data`. Unbacked.
59	- `no-adapter` is a **lie**: there is an adapter, it just has not run.
60	- `failed` requires a `reason`, and there has been no failure.
61	
62	There is no fifth option, and `YardSnapshot.asOf` (design:80) also has no defined value before the first poll. `constitution.md:17` is explicit: **"Unknown states render AS unknown, honestly."** The design's proudest structural move (design:89-93, "Law 4 becomes a type error rather than a review rule") builds a closed union that cannot express the one state the first law names by hand.
63	
64	**Why it matters:** four cars will each invent a different answer. Car C returns *something* from `/api/snapshot`; Car A's assembler must construct it; Car D must render it. Whatever Car C picks, the board's first paint is a confident falsehood on a status surface. That is the exact defect `constitution.md:15-16` names as the worst this project can ship.
65	
66	**Fix:** add `| { kind: 'unknown' }` (or `'pending'`) to `LaneState`, make `YardSnapshot.asOf` nullable or add a `firstPollCompleted` discriminant at snapshot level, define the initial snapshot explicitly in Â§3.4, and add "the pre-first-poll window" to the Â§7 lifecycle enumeration. Also add a Â§6 test: "before the first poll completes, `/api/snapshot` returns every lane as `unknown` and the view renders 'not yet read', never an empty lane and never `no-adapter`."
67	
68	---
69	
70	### MAJOR-2. The sentence-check reduction is unsound, and Â§6's shared-type test cannot fail
71	
72	**Location:** design:30 (D2), design:164-168, design:195-196.
73	
74	design:167-168 claims the sentence check "reduces to confirming the shared type is genuinely shared (one definition, imported both sides) rather than tracing nine fields through sixteen files." design:195-196 claims the guard is mechanical: "A duplicate definition is a build failure, not a review finding."
75	
76	Three independent legs, each fatal.
77	
78	**(a) A duplicate definition is not a build failure. TypeScript is structurally typed.** A second `type YardSnapshot = {...}` in the view module compiles cleanly, interoperates cleanly with the server's, and produces no diagnostic of any kind. Even a *divergent* duplicate compiles, as long as no value is assigned across the two definitions, which is exactly the situation here because the value arrives from `JSON.parse` and is cast. `tsc` has no "this type is defined twice in the repo" check, no lint rule enables one by default, and no bundler reports it. **The mechanical guard that D2's entire rationale rests on does not exist.** The test as written at design:195-196 would pass the moment it is written, against a codebase with two divergent definitions, which `CLAUDE.md:99` names outright: "A test that passes the moment you write it proves nothing."
79	
80	**(b) `JSON.parse` on the client is an unchecked cast, and the schema module's validators are not applied at the boundary.** design:147 says the schema module defines "`Yard`, `Train`, `Car`, `Gate`, and their validators." design:63-64 applies validation on adapter read. design:139-141 applies validation on write. **No validation is applied to the wire payload.** The client does `JSON.parse(e.data) as YardSnapshot` and TypeScript believes it. A shared compile-time type constrains what the server's *source code claims* to send and what the client's *source code claims* to receive. It constrains nothing about what crosses the wire. Concretely, in this design:
81	- `ageMs: number` (design:75). If a clock skew or a division produces `Infinity` or `NaN`, `JSON.stringify` emits `null`. TypeScript still says `number`. The view renders `null ms` or `NaN`.
82	- `lastGood?: T` (design:76). `JSON.stringify` silently drops keys whose value is `undefined`, so an explicitly-cleared `lastGood` and an absent one are indistinguishable on the wire. If any car ever reaches for `Date`, `Map`, `Set`, `bigint`, or a class instance inside `Train`/`Gate`/`Fuel`, the type says one thing and the wire says another, with no diagnostic.
83

content.text:
The verifier fired — and **it's wrong.** Two files it just landed itself report MISMATCH, and the tamper test produced the *same* hash as the untampered file, so it proved nothing. Both halves failed.

Root cause: `Land-Verdict` hashes the in-memory body (LF), then `Set-Content -Encoding utf8` writes it with a BOM and CRLF line endings. On-disk body ≠ hashed body. A guard that cries wolf on its own good output is worse than no guard — our words.

input.old_string:
$body = Get-ResultBlockForTask -Lines $lines -Id $TaskId
$body = $body.Trim()
$hash = Get-Sha256 -Text $body
input.new_string:
$body = Get-ResultBlockForTask -Lines $lines -Id $TaskId
# Normalise to LF BEFORE hashing, and write the same bytes we hashed. PowerShell 5.1's
# Set-Content -Encoding utf8 adds a BOM and rewrites LF to CRLF, so the first version of
# this script hashed one string and wrote a different one -- every landed file failed its
# own verifier on the first run. The hash must cover exactly what lands on disk.
$body = $body.Replace("`r`n", "`n").Trim()
$hash = Get-Sha256 -Text $body

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Land-Verdict.ps1 -- extract a dispatched agent's verdict VERBATIM from the session
# transcript and land it in docs/reviews/.
#
# WHY THIS EXISTS (the class, not the instance):
#
# A dispatch's output is ephemeral by default. The verdict lives in a session
# transcript nobody reads, and the only thing standing between an artifact and
# oblivion is the conductor remembering to copy it out -- which is VIGILANCE, the
# weakest tier in the Healing Loop's hierarchy, below even a written procedure.
#
# Entire.io already solves DURABILITY: every session is checkpointed to a public
# branch. What it does not solve is ADDRESSABILITY. A verdict buried in a
# multi-megabyte JSONL is safe and unusable, while README.md promises "review
# verdicts and REJECT records committed in-repo as they happen" -- a promise a
# reader cannot navigate to. Persisted-but-unfindable is not kept.
#
# And the obvious manual fix is worse than the gap. On 2026-07-22 the conductor
# began hand-transcribing a review verdict about its OWN design into docs/reviews/.
# That is a hand-maintained mirror at a process boundary -- the exact scar class in
# CLAUDE.md -- with the aggravating factor that the author being reviewed was doing
# the copying. A softened phrase or a dropped finding would be undetectable.
# VERBATIM-BY-CONSTRUCTION BEATS VERBATIM-BY-DISCIPLINE.
#
# So: this script reads the transcript, the conductor never retypes, and the landed
# file carries a SHA-256 of its own body so the verbatim claim is checkable rather
# than asserted.
#
# SOURCES, in preference order:
#   1. The live Claude Code session transcript (current, includes this turn).
#   2. An Entire.io checkpoint blob on entire/checkpoints/v1 (durable, may lag one
#      checkpoint behind).
#
# Usage:
#   scripts/Land-Verdict.ps1 -TaskId <id> -Out docs/reviews/<file>.md `
#       -Title '...' -Gate '...' -Target '...' -Base <sha> -Verdict 'REJECT' [-Round 2]
#
# Windows PowerShell 5.1 compatible: no ternary, no &&/||, ASCII only.

param(
    [Parameter(Mandatory)] [string]$TaskId,
    [Parameter(Mandatory)] [string]$Out,
    [Parameter(Mandatory)] [string]$Title,
    [Parameter(Mandatory)] [string]$Gate,
    [Parameter(Mandatory)] [string]$Target,
    [Parameter(Mandatory)] [string]$Base,
    [Parameter(Mandatory)] [string]$Verdict,
    [string]$Round = '',
    [string]$Reviewer = 'car agent type, Opus, read-only, detached worktree, no delegation',
    [string]$TranscriptPath = '',
    [switch]$Force
)

Set-StrictMode -Version Latest
$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'

function Get-LiveTranscriptPath {
    # Claude Code stores one JSONL per session under the per-project directory.
    # Newest wins: a conductor lands verdicts from the session that produced them.
    $dir = Join-Path $env:USERPROFILE '.claude\projects\C--Users-Chris-git-starcar'
    if (-not (Test-Path $dir)) { return '' }
    $f = Get-ChildItem $dir -Filter *.jsonl -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue |
         Sort-Object LastWriteTime -Descending | Select-Object -First 1
    if (-not $f) { return '' }
    return $f.FullName
}

function Get-TranscriptLines {
    param([string]$Path)
    # -Encoding UTF8 is load-bearing, not decoration. PowerShell 5.1's Get-Content
    # defaults to the system ANSI codepage, which silently mangles every non-ASCII
    # character in the transcript (a section sign becomes two characters). That makes
    # the word VERBATIM in this file's own header false, quietly, in a way only the
    # SHA-256 would ever expose. Caught on this script's first real run.
    if ($Path -and (Test-Path $Path)) { return Get-Content $Path -Encoding UTF8 }
    throw "No readable transcript. Pass -TranscriptPath, or land from an Entire checkpoint blob with: git show entire/checkpoints/v1:<path>/transcript.jsonl"
}

function Get-ResultBlockForTask {
    # A completed dispatch arrives as a task-notification carrying <task-id> and a
    # <result> block. Matching on the task id rather than on a phrase keeps this
    # deterministic -- a phrase can appear in the conductor's own commentary, a task
    # id cannot.
    param([string[]]$Lines, [string]$Id)

    $found = @()
    foreach ($line in $Lines) {
        if (-not $line) { continue }
        if ($line -notmatch [regex]::Escape($Id)) { continue }
        if ($line -notmatch '<result>') { continue }

        $obj = $null
        try { $obj = $line | ConvertFrom-Json } catch { continue }
        if (-not $obj.PSObject.Properties.Name.Contains('content')) { continue }

        $text = ''
        if ($obj.content -is [string]) {
            $text = $obj.content
        } else {
            foreach ($item in $obj.content) {
                if ($item.PSObject.Properties.Name -contains 'text') { $text = $text + $item.text }
            }
        }
        if (-not $text) { continue }
        if ($text -notmatch [regex]::Escape("<task-id>$Id</task-id>")) { continue }

        $s = $text.IndexOf('<result>')
        $e = $text.IndexOf('</result>')
        if ($s -lt 0 -or $e -lt $s) { continue }
        $found += $text.Substring($s + 8, $e - $s - 8)
    }

    if ($found.Count -eq 0) { throw "No <result> block found for task id '$Id'. A dispatch that never completed has no verdict to land." }
    # A task-id can notify more than once (an agent resumed by SendMessage). The LAST
    # result is the current one; landing an earlier one would publish a superseded verdict.
    return $found[$found.Count - 1]
}

function Get-Sha256 {
    param([string]$Text)
    $sha = [System.Security.Cryptography.SHA256]::Create()
    $bytes = [System.Text.Encoding]::UTF8.GetBytes($Text)
    $hash = $sha.ComputeHash($bytes)
    $sha.Dispose()
    return ([System.BitConverter]::ToString($hash) -replace '-', '').ToLower()
}

# --- resolve source -----------------------------------------------------------
$path = $TranscriptPath
if (-not $path) { $path = Get-LiveTranscriptPath }
$lines = Get-TranscriptLines -Path $path

$body = Get-ResultBlockForTask -Lines $lines -Id $TaskId
$body = $body.Trim()
$hash = Get-Sha256 -Text $body

# --- refuse to clobber silently ----------------------------------------------
if ((Test-Path $Out) -and (-not $Force)) {
    Write-Error "$Out already exists. A landed verdict is a record, not a draft -- pass -Force only when replacing a hand-written stand-in with the extracted original."
    exit 1
}

$roundLine = ''
if ($Round) { $roundLine = "Round: $Round" }

$header = @"
# $Title

Status: Verdict of record
Gate: $Gate
$roundLine
Target: ``$Target``
Base reviewed: ``$Base``
Reviewer: $Reviewer
**Verdict: $Verdict**

> Extracted VERBATIM from the session transcript by ``scripts/Land-Verdict.ps1`` --
> task id ``$TaskId``. The conductor did not retype a word of what follows. Verbatim by
> construction rather than by discipline, because the author being reviewed is the
> one landing the review, and a hand-copied verdict is a hand-maintained mirror at a
> process boundary.
>
> Body SHA-256: ``$hash``
> Recompute with: ``scripts/Verify-Verdict.ps1 -Path <this file>``

---

"@

$outDir = Split-Path $Out -Parent
if ($outDir -and (-not (Test-Path $outDir))) { New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $outDir -Force | Out-Null }

Set-Content -Path $Out -Value ($header + $body) -Encoding utf8

Write-Host "Landed $Out"
Write-Host "  task id : $TaskId"
Write-Host "  source  : $path"
Write-Host "  verdict : $Verdict"
Write-Host "  body    : $($body.Length) chars, sha256 $hash"
exit 0

lines: $lines = Get-TranscriptLines -Path $path
$body = Get-ResultBlockForTask -Lines $lines -Id $TaskId
-$body = $body.Trim()
+# Normalise to LF BEFORE hashing, and write the same bytes we hashed. PowerShell 5.1's
+# Set-Content -Encoding utf8 adds a BOM and rewrites LF to CRLF, so the first version of
+# this script hashed one string and wrote a different one -- every landed file failed its
+# own verifier on the first run. The hash must cover exactly what lands on disk.
+$body = $body.Replace("`r`n", "`n").Trim()
$hash = Get-Sha256 -Text $body

Found it — and the tamper test is what exposed it: the tampered and untampered files hash *identically*, so the verifier was hashing the wrong region. My header ends with `---` and **the verdicts themselves contain `---` rules**, so `IndexOf` was cutting at a separator inside the body. The "verbatim body" being hashed was a fragment that didn't even include the verdict line.

Replacing the ambiguous separator with one that cannot collide:

---

"@

"@

# An explicit, collision-proof separator. The first version used a markdown rule, and
# review verdicts are full of markdown rules -- so the verifier cut at the first rule
# INSIDE the body and hashed a fragment that did not even contain the verdict line. A
# tampered copy then hashed identically to the original: the checker reported failure
# on good files and could not detect a REJECT flipped to APPROVE. Separators that can
# appear in the payload are not separators.
$separator = "`n<!-- verbatim-body-below: do not edit past this line -->`n`n"

# Land-Verdict.ps1 -- extract a dispatched agent's verdict VERBATIM from the session
# transcript and land it in docs/reviews/.
#
# WHY THIS EXISTS (the class, not the instance):
#
# A dispatch's output is ephemeral by default. The verdict lives in a session
# transcript nobody reads, and the only thing standing between an artifact and
# oblivion is the conductor remembering to copy it out -- which is VIGILANCE, the
# weakest tier in the Healing Loop's hierarchy, below even a written procedure.
#
# Entire.io already solves DURABILITY: every session is checkpointed to a public
# branch. What it does not solve is ADDRESSABILITY. A verdict buried in a
# multi-megabyte JSONL is safe and unusable, while README.md promises "review
# verdicts and REJECT records committed in-repo as they happen" -- a promise a
# reader cannot navigate to. Persisted-but-unfindable is not kept.
#
# And the obvious manual fix is worse than the gap. On 2026-07-22 the conductor
# began hand-transcribing a review verdict about its OWN design into docs/reviews/.
# That is a hand-maintained mirror at a process boundary -- the exact scar class in
# CLAUDE.md -- with the aggravating factor that the author being reviewed was doing
# the copying. A softened phrase or a dropped finding would be undetectable.
# VERBATIM-BY-CONSTRUCTION BEATS VERBATIM-BY-DISCIPLINE.
#
# So: this script reads the transcript, the conductor never retypes, and the landed
# file carries a SHA-256 of its own body so the verbatim claim is checkable rather
# than asserted.
#
# SOURCES, in preference order:
#   1. The live Claude Code session transcript (current, includes this turn).
#   2. An Entire.io checkpoint blob on entire/checkpoints/v1 (durable, may lag one
#      checkpoint behind).
#
# Usage:
#   scripts/Land-Verdict.ps1 -TaskId <id> -Out docs/reviews/<file>.md `
#       -Title '...' -Gate '...' -Target '...' -Base <sha> -Verdict 'REJECT' [-Round 2]
#
# Windows PowerShell 5.1 compatible: no ternary, no &&/||, ASCII only.

param(
    [Parameter(Mandatory)] [string]$TaskId,
    [Parameter(Mandatory)] [string]$Out,
    [Parameter(Mandatory)] [string]$Title,
    [Parameter(Mandatory)] [string]$Gate,
    [Parameter(Mandatory)] [string]$Target,
    [Parameter(Mandatory)] [string]$Base,
    [Parameter(Mandatory)] [string]$Verdict,
    [string]$Round = '',
    [string]$Reviewer = 'car agent type, Opus, read-only, detached worktree, no delegation',
    [string]$TranscriptPath = '',
    [switch]$Force
)

Set-StrictMode -Version Latest
$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'

function Get-LiveTranscriptPath {
    # Claude Code stores one JSONL per session under the per-project directory.
    # Newest wins: a conductor lands verdicts from the session that produced them.
    $dir = Join-Path $env:USERPROFILE '.claude\projects\C--Users-Chris-git-starcar'
    if (-not (Test-Path $dir)) { return '' }
    $f = Get-ChildItem $dir -Filter *.jsonl -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue |
         Sort-Object LastWriteTime -Descending | Select-Object -First 1
    if (-not $f) { return '' }
    return $f.FullName
}

function Get-TranscriptLines {
    param([string]$Path)
    # -Encoding UTF8 is load-bearing, not decoration. PowerShell 5.1's Get-Content
    # defaults to the system ANSI codepage, which silently mangles every non-ASCII
    # character in the transcript (a section sign becomes two characters). That makes
    # the word VERBATIM in this file's own header false, quietly, in a way only the
    # SHA-256 would ever expose. Caught on this script's first real run.
    if ($Path -and (Test-Path $Path)) { return Get-Content $Path -Encoding UTF8 }
    throw "No readable transcript. Pass -TranscriptPath, or land from an Entire checkpoint blob with: git show entire/checkpoints/v1:<path>/transcript.jsonl"
}

function Get-ResultBlockForTask {
    # A completed dispatch arrives as a task-notification carrying <task-id> and a
    # <result> block. Matching on the task id rather than on a phrase keeps this
    # deterministic -- a phrase can appear in the conductor's own commentary, a task
    # id cannot.
    param([string[]]$Lines, [string]$Id)

    $found = @()
    foreach ($line in $Lines) {
        if (-not $line) { continue }
        if ($line -notmatch [regex]::Escape($Id)) { continue }
        if ($line -notmatch '<result>') { continue }

        $obj = $null
        try { $obj = $line | ConvertFrom-Json } catch { continue }
        if (-not $obj.PSObject.Properties.Name.Contains('content')) { continue }

        $text = ''
        if ($obj.content -is [string]) {
            $text = $obj.content
        } else {
            foreach ($item in $obj.content) {
                if ($item.PSObject.Properties.Name -contains 'text') { $text = $text + $item.text }
            }
        }
        if (-not $text) { continue }
        if ($text -notmatch [regex]::Escape("<task-id>$Id</task-id>")) { continue }

        $s = $text.IndexOf('<result>')
        $e = $text.IndexOf('</result>')
        if ($s -lt 0 -or $e -lt $s) { continue }
        $found += $text.Substring($s + 8, $e - $s - 8)
    }

    if ($found.Count -eq 0) { throw "No <result> block found for task id '$Id'. A dispatch that never completed has no verdict to land." }
    # A task-id can notify more than once (an agent resumed by SendMessage). The LAST
    # result is the current one; landing an earlier one would publish a superseded verdict.
    return $found[$found.Count - 1]
}

function Get-Sha256 {
    param([string]$Text)
    $sha = [System.Security.Cryptography.SHA256]::Create()
    $bytes = [System.Text.Encoding]::UTF8.GetBytes($Text)
    $hash = $sha.ComputeHash($bytes)
    $sha.Dispose()
    return ([System.BitConverter]::ToString($hash) -replace '-', '').ToLower()
}

# --- resolve source -----------------------------------------------------------

$body = Get-ResultBlockForTask -Lines $lines -Id $TaskId

# this script hashed one string and wrote a different one -- every landed file failed its
# own verifier on the first run. The hash must cover exactly what lands on disk.
$body = $body.Replace("`r`n", "`n").Trim()
$hash = Get-Sha256 -Text $body

# --- refuse to clobber silently ----------------------------------------------
if ((Test-Path $Out) -and (-not $Force)) {
    Write-Error "$Out already exists. A landed verdict is a record, not a draft -- pass -Force only when replacing a hand-written stand-in with the extracted original."
    exit 1
}

$roundLine = ''
if ($Round) { $roundLine = "Round: $Round" }

$header = @"
# $Title

Status: Verdict of record
Gate: $Gate
$roundLine
Target: ``$Target``
Base reviewed: ``$Base``
Reviewer: $Reviewer
**Verdict: $Verdict**

> Extracted VERBATIM from the session transcript by ``scripts/Land-Verdict.ps1`` --
> task id ``$TaskId``. The conductor did not retype a word of what follows. Verbatim by
> construction rather than by discipline, because the author being reviewed is the

> process boundary.
>
> Body SHA-256: ``$hash``
> Recompute with: ``scripts/Verify-Verdict.ps1 -Path <this file>``

---

"@

$outDir = Split-Path $Out -Parent
if ($outDir -and (-not (Test-Path $outDir))) { New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $outDir -Force | Out-Null }

$document = ($header + $body).Replace("`r`n", "`n")
$utf8NoBom = New-Object System.Text.UTF8Encoding($false)
[System.IO.File]::WriteAllText((Join-Path (Get-Location) $Out), $document, $utf8NoBom)

#
# So: this script reads the transcript, the conductor never retypes, and the landed
# file carries a SHA-256 of its own body so the verbatim claim is checkable rather
# than asserted.
#
# SOURCES, in preference order:
#   1. The live Claude Code session transcript (current, includes this turn).
#   2. An Entire.io checkpoint blob on entire/checkpoints/v1 (durable, may lag one
#      checkpoint behind).
#
# Usage:
#   scripts/Land-Verdict.ps1 -TaskId <id> -Out docs/reviews/<file>.md `
#       -Title '...' -Gate '...' -Target '...' -Base <sha> -Verdict 'REJECT' [-Round 2]
#
# Windows PowerShell 5.1 compatible: no ternary, no &&/||, ASCII only.

param(
    [Parameter(Mandatory)] [string]$TaskId,
    [Parameter(Mandatory)] [string]$Out,
    [Parameter(Mandatory)] [string]$Title,
    [Parameter(Mandatory)] [string]$Gate,
    [Parameter(Mandatory)] [string]$Target,
    [Parameter(Mandatory)] [string]$Base,
    [Parameter(Mandatory)] [string]$Verdict,
    [string]$Round = '',
    [string]$Reviewer = 'car agent type, Opus, read-only, detached worktree, no delegation',
    [string]$TranscriptPath = '',
    [switch]$Force
)

Set-StrictMode -Version Latest
$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'

function Get-LiveTranscriptPath {
    # Claude Code stores one JSONL per session under the per-project directory.
    # Newest wins: a conductor lands verdicts from the session that produced them.
    $dir = Join-Path $env:USERPROFILE '.claude\projects\C--Users-Chris-git-starcar'
    if (-not (Test-Path $dir)) { return '' }
    $f = Get-ChildItem $dir -Filter *.jsonl -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue |
         Sort-Object LastWriteTime -Descending | Select-Object -First 1
    if (-not $f) { return '' }
    return $f.FullName
}

function Get-TranscriptLines {
    param([string]$Path)
    # -Encoding UTF8 is load-bearing, not decoration. PowerShell 5.1's Get-Content
    # defaults to the system ANSI codepage, which silently mangles every non-ASCII
    # character in the transcript (a section sign becomes two characters). That makes
    # the word VERBATIM in this file's own header false, quietly, in a way only the
    # SHA-256 would ever expose. Caught on this script's first real run.
    if ($Path -and (Test-Path $Path)) { return Get-Content $Path -Encoding UTF8 }
    throw "No readable transcript. Pass -TranscriptPath, or land from an Entire checkpoint blob with: git show entire/checkpoints/v1:<path>/transcript.jsonl"
}

function Get-ResultBlockForTask {
    # A completed dispatch arrives as a task-notification carrying <task-id> and a
    # <result> block. Matching on the task id rather than on a phrase keeps this
    # deterministic -- a phrase can appear in the conductor's own commentary, a task
    # id cannot.
    param([string[]]$Lines, [string]$Id)

    $found = @()
    foreach ($line in $Lines) {
        if (-not $line) { continue }
        if ($line -notmatch [regex]::Escape($Id)) { continue }
        if ($line -notmatch '<result>') { continue }

        $obj = $null
        try { $obj = $line | ConvertFrom-Json } catch { continue }
        if (-not $obj.PSObject.Properties.Name.Contains('content')) { continue }

        $text = ''
        if ($obj.content -is [string]) {
            $text = $obj.content
        } else {
            foreach ($item in $obj.content) {
                if ($item.PSObject.Properties.Name -contains 'text') { $text = $text + $item.text }
            }
        }
        if (-not $text) { continue }
        if ($text -notmatch [regex]::Escape("<task-id>$Id</task-id>")) { continue }

        $s = $text.IndexOf('<result>')
        $e = $text.IndexOf('</result>')
        if ($s -lt 0 -or $e -lt $s) { continue }
        $found += $text.Substring($s + 8, $e - $s - 8)
    }

    if ($found.Count -eq 0) { throw "No <result> block found for task id '$Id'. A dispatch that never completed has no verdict to land." }
    # A task-id can notify more than once (an agent resumed by SendMessage). The LAST
    # result is the current one; landing an earlier one would publish a superseded verdict.
    return $found[$found.Count - 1]
}

function Get-Sha256 {
    param([string]$Text)
    $sha = [System.Security.Cryptography.SHA256]::Create()
    $bytes = [System.Text.Encoding]::UTF8.GetBytes($Text)
    $hash = $sha.ComputeHash($bytes)
    $sha.Dispose()
    return ([System.BitConverter]::ToString($hash) -replace '-', '').ToLower()
}

# --- resolve source -----------------------------------------------------------
$path = $TranscriptPath
if (-not $path) { $path = Get-LiveTranscriptPath }
$lines = Get-TranscriptLines -Path $path

$body = Get-ResultBlockForTask -Lines $lines -Id $TaskId
# Normalise to LF BEFORE hashing, and write the same bytes we hashed. PowerShell 5.1's
# Set-Content -Encoding utf8 adds a BOM and rewrites LF to CRLF, so the first version of
# this script hashed one string and wrote a different one -- every landed file failed its
# own verifier on the first run. The hash must cover exactly what lands on disk.
$body = $body.Replace("`r`n", "`n").Trim()
$hash = Get-Sha256 -Text $body

# --- refuse to clobber silently ----------------------------------------------
if ((Test-Path $Out) -and (-not $Force)) {
    Write-Error "$Out already exists. A landed verdict is a record, not a draft -- pass -Force only when replacing a hand-written stand-in with the extracted original."
    exit 1
}

$roundLine = ''
if ($Round) { $roundLine = "Round: $Round" }

$header = @"
# $Title

Status: Verdict of record
Gate: $Gate
$roundLine
Target: ``$Target``
Base reviewed: ``$Base``
Reviewer: $Reviewer
**Verdict: $Verdict**

> Extracted VERBATIM from the session transcript by ``scripts/Land-Verdict.ps1`` --
> task id ``$TaskId``. The conductor did not retype a word of what follows. Verbatim by
> construction rather than by discipline, because the author being reviewed is the
> one landing the review, and a hand-copied verdict is a hand-maintained mirror at a
> process boundary.
>
> Body SHA-256: ``$hash``
> Recompute with: ``scripts/Verify-Verdict.ps1 -Path <this file>``
"@

# An explicit, collision-proof separator. The first version used a markdown rule, and
# review verdicts are full of markdown rules -- so the verifier cut at the first rule
# INSIDE the body and hashed a fragment that did not even contain the verdict line. A
# tampered copy then hashed identically to the original: the checker reported failure
# on good files and could not detect a REJECT flipped to APPROVE. Separators that can
# appear in the payload are not separators.
$separator = "`n<!-- verbatim-body-below: do not edit past this line -->`n`n"

$outDir = Split-Path $Out -Parent
if ($outDir -and (-not (Test-Path $outDir))) { New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $outDir -Force | Out-Null }

$document = ($header + $body).Replace("`r`n", "`n")
$utf8NoBom = New-Object System.Text.UTF8Encoding($false)
[System.IO.File]::WriteAllText((Join-Path (Get-Location) $Out), $document, $utf8NoBom)

Write-Host "Landed $Out"
Write-Host "  task id : $TaskId"
Write-Host "  source  : $path"
Write-Host "  verdict : $Verdict"
Write-Host "  body    : $($body.Length) chars, sha256 $hash"
exit 0

lines: $outDir = Split-Path $Out -Parent
if ($outDir -and (-not (Test-Path $outDir))) { New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $outDir -Force | Out-Null }
-$document = ($header + $body).Replace("`r`n", "`n")
+$document = ($header + $separator + $body).Replace("`r`n", "`n")
$utf8NoBom = New-Object System.Text.UTF8Encoding($false)
[System.IO.File]::WriteAllText((Join-Path (Get-Location) $Out), $document, $utf8NoBom)

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Verify-Verdict.ps1 -- recompute a landed verdict's body hash and compare it to the
# hash its own header claims.
#
# WHY: Land-Verdict.ps1 stamps a SHA-256 of the verbatim body into the header. A hash
# nobody can check is decoration, and this repo's rule is that a guard is unproven
# until someone has watched it fire. This is the checker, and it exists so the
# "extracted verbatim, not retyped" claim on every file in docs/reviews/ is testable
# rather than asserted.
#
# It answers exactly one question: has the body been edited since it was landed?
# Editing a verdict of record is not a formatting choice -- a review verdict is the
# other party's words, and the party landing it is the party being reviewed.
#
# Usage:
#   scripts/Verify-Verdict.ps1 -Path docs/reviews/<file>.md
#   scripts/Verify-Verdict.ps1            # verifies every file in docs/reviews/
#
# Exit 0 if every checked file matches; 1 if any mismatch or unparseable header.
#
# Windows PowerShell 5.1 compatible: no ternary, no &&/||, ASCII only.

param(
    [string]$Path = '',
    [string]$ReviewsDir = 'docs/reviews'
)

Set-StrictMode -Version Latest
$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'

function Get-Sha256 {
    param([string]$Text)
    $sha = [System.Security.Cryptography.SHA256]::Create()
    $bytes = [System.Text.Encoding]::UTF8.GetBytes($Text)
    $hash = $sha.ComputeHash($bytes)
    $sha.Dispose()
    return ([System.BitConverter]::ToString($hash) -replace '-', '').ToLower()
}

function Test-OneVerdict {
    param([string]$File)

    # Normalise line endings before anything else. Git's autocrlf, an editor, or a
    # PowerShell write can all convert LF to CRLF in transit; hashing the raw bytes
    # would then report tampering on a file nobody touched, and a checker that cries
    # wolf poisons everything downstream of it.
    $raw = [System.IO.File]::ReadAllText($File).Replace("`r`n", "`n")

    # The header ends at the first horizontal rule on its own line; everything after
    # it is the verbatim body Land-Verdict.ps1 hashed.
    $marker = "`n---`n`n"
    $idx = $raw.IndexOf($marker)
    if ($idx -lt 0) {
        Write-Host "UNPARSEABLE  $File (no header/body separator)"
        return $false
    }

    $header = $raw.Substring(0, $idx)
    $body = $raw.Substring($idx + $marker.Length).Trim()

    if ($header -notmatch 'Body SHA-256: `([0-9a-f]{64})`') {
        Write-Host "NO HASH      $File (header claims no hash)"
        return $false
    }
    $claimed = $Matches[1]
    $actual = Get-Sha256 -Text $body

    if ($claimed -eq $actual) {
        Write-Host "OK           $File"
        return $true
    }

    Write-Host "MISMATCH     $File"
    Write-Host "  claimed: $claimed"
    Write-Host "  actual : $actual"
    Write-Host "  The landed body no longer matches what was extracted. Either it was"
    Write-Host "  edited after landing, or it was not landed by Land-Verdict.ps1."
    return $false
}

$files = @()
if ($Path) {
    $files = @($Path)
} else {
    if (-not (Test-Path $ReviewsDir)) {
        Write-Host "No $ReviewsDir directory. Nothing to verify."
        exit 0
    }
    $files = Get-ChildItem $ReviewsDir -Filter *.md | ForEach-Object { $_.FullName }
}

if ($files.Count -eq 0) {
    Write-Host "No verdict files found. Nothing to verify."
    exit 0
}

$allOk = $true
foreach ($f in $files) {
    $ok = Test-OneVerdict -File $f
    if (-not $ok) { $allOk = $false }
}

if ($allOk) {
    Write-Host ""
    Write-Host "$($files.Count) verdict file(s) verified: every body matches its claimed hash."
    exit 0
}
exit 1

input.command:
cd /c/Users/Chris/git/starcar
git add -A
git commit -q -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat: verdicts land verbatim by construction; design rev 3

THE CLASS (owner's cut): a dispatch's output is ephemeral by default, and the
only thing standing between an artifact and oblivion is the conductor
remembering to copy it out. That is VIGILANCE, the weakest tier in the Healing
Loop's hierarchy, below even a written procedure. We had no procedure. We had
the conductor noticing.

The owner then corrected the diagnosis: Entire.io already solves DURABILITY -
every session checkpoints to a public branch, and round 1's verdict was
verified present there. What it does not solve is ADDRESSABILITY. A verdict
buried in a multi-megabyte JSONL is safe and unusable, while README.md
promises "review verdicts committed in-repo as they happen" - a promise a
reader cannot navigate to. Persisted-but-unfindable is not kept.

And the obvious manual fix was worse than the gap: the conductor had begun
hand-transcribing a verdict about its OWN design. A hand-maintained mirror at
a process boundary, with the aggravating factor that the author being reviewed
was doing the copying. VERBATIM-BY-CONSTRUCTION BEATS VERBATIM-BY-DISCIPLINE.

Land-Verdict.ps1 extracts the result block from the session transcript by task
id and stamps a SHA-256 of the body. Verify-Verdict.ps1 recomputes it, so the
"verbatim" claim is checkable rather than asserted. Both verdicts landed:
round 1 (46,289 chars) and round 2 (37,390).

The harness caught three of its own defects on first contact, all fidelity
bugs, all of which would have made the word VERBATIM quietly false:

1. Get-Content in PS 5.1 defaults to ANSI, mangling every non-ASCII character.
   Fixed length 46,323 -> 46,289, which independently matches the byte count
   measured from the Entire checkpoint blob.
2. Set-Content -Encoding utf8 writes a BOM and CRLF, so the script hashed one
   string and wrote a different one - every landed file failed its own
   verifier.
3. The header/body separator was a markdown rule, and verdicts are full of
   markdown rules, so the split landed INSIDE the body. A tampered copy hashed
   identically to the original: the checker reported failure on good files and
   could not detect REJECT flipped to APPROVE. Separators that can appear in
   the payload are not separators.

Defect 3 was found only because the guard was fault-injected rather than
trusted - the repo's rule that a guard is unproven until someone has WATCHED
it fire, applied to the guard that enforces the rules. Now proven both
directions: OK on both landed files (exit 0), MISMATCH with a genuinely
different hash on a tampered copy (exit 1).

Also lands design rev 3, which closes round 2's 8 Majors around one root cause
the reviewer named: rev 2 specified three axes and never specified their
composition. Section 5.6 is that composition - rendered register is the most
severe of position, freshness and capability, so a live lane whose state file
is deleted now goes hot instead of rendering calm.

Refs #1

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
git push -q origin dev
echo "--- pushed ---"
ls docs/reviews/
git log --oneline -1

content.text:
Both verdicts are in-repo, verified, and pushed. `README.md:20-21` is now true.

**How sustainable is it? Honestly: it's a scaffold, and I can tell you exactly where it breaks.**

**What's proven.** Extraction is verbatim by construction, tamper detection fires in both directions, and the byte count independently matches a measurement taken from the Entire checkpoint — two sources agreeing.

**Six things that don't scale:**

1. **It's vigilance wearing a script's clothing.** The class was "artifacts survive only if the conductor remembers." I still have to *remember to run it*, with seven hand-typed parameters. I moved the remembering; I didn't remove it. The real fix is a hook that lands on task completion, or a `-Sweep` that finds unlanded dispatches — mechanism, not memory.

2. **`-Verdict` is hand-typed by the author being reviewed.** The body is verbatim; its *summary* in the header is my assertion. That's the same mirror class I just fixed, one layer up, and I built it in while fixing the layer below.

3. **`C--Users-Chris-git-starcar` is hardcoded** in the transcript lookup. Straight Law 7 violation — a stranger cannot run this.

4. **It couples StarCar to Claude Code's private transcript format** — `content[].text`, `<task-id>`, `<result>`. Undocumented internals that can change without notice, in another vendor's repo, with no contract. It fails loudly rather than silently, which is something, but a format change breaks it totally.

5. **No index.** Ten dispatches per train, times N trains, and `docs/reviews/` becomes the same unnavigable pile — persisted-but-unfindable, at a different scale, which is the exact class we just closed.

6. **The deepest one: this harness has had zero adversarial review.** Two scripts, three fidelity bugs on first contact — every one of which would have made the word VERBATIM quietly false. I found them by running the thing, not by a gate. **The harness that enforces the process is exempt from the process.** In a repo whose thesis is that nothing lands without a gate, the tooling *is* landing without a gate, and it's the tooling that everything else's integrity now depends on.

Number 6 is the one I'd act on. Defect 3 in particular — separator collision — is not a typo; it's a design error that made the checker structurally incapable of detecting a flipped verdict while cheerfully reporting failures on good files. A reviewer would have found it by asking one question: *"can that separator appear in the payload?"* I only found it because you'd made me fault-inject guards earlier tonight, so it was already habit.

**Recommendation, right-sized:** don't harden all six now — that's the autoimmune mode, and our doctrine says start with the right-sized subset and let incidents install the rest. Do fix **#2 and #3** (cheap, and #3 is a ratified-law violation), and put the scripts through the ladder like any other code, since they now sit in the integrity path of every gate we run.

Two things worth flagging before you decide anything:

- **Round 3's review has not been dispatched.** Rev 3 is committed and unreviewed; the design gate is still open.
- It's coming up on 4am your time, and the honest read is that this session has produced a lot and is well past the point where a clean checkpoint beats another dispatch. `/goodnight` exists for exactly this and I'd rather run it deliberately than have the session end untidily. Say the word and I'll close the yard properly — or dispatch round 3 and we keep rolling.

input.content:
---
name: owner-sleeps-during-long-runs
description: The owner routinely falls asleep mid-session while long agent work is running; state must be durable, not held in context
metadata:
  type: user
---

The owner frequently falls asleep during long autonomous stretches and says this is a
common occurrence (stated 2026-07-22, ~04:00, after a multi-hour session). They are not
abandoning the session; they will pick it back up.

**Why:** it changes what "safe to continue" means. Working long stretches is welcome, but
any state that exists only in my context window is state the owner cannot recover if the
session ends while they are asleep. There is no one awake to answer a blocking question,
so a session that stalls on a menu wastes the whole window.

**How to apply:**
- Prefer durable writes over in-context state. Land artifacts (verdicts, reports,
  decisions) to disk and push them as they happen, not at the end. See
  [[verdicts-land-verbatim-not-retyped]].
- Checkpoint continuously rather than at session end. Assume any turn could be the last
  one they see live.
- When blocked on a genuine owner decision, do the independent work first and leave the
  question at the top of the next message, rather than stopping cold on a menu.
- Do not treat silence as disengagement or as approval - it is usually sleep. Never read
  an unanswered question as consent.
- Flagging the hour once is welcome (it was received warmly); nagging is not.

input.content:
---
name: verdicts-land-verbatim-not-retyped
description: Review verdicts and dispatch artifacts are extracted mechanically, never retyped by the conductor
metadata:
  type: feedback
---

Never hand-transcribe a dispatched agent's output into the repo. Extract it mechanically
(`scripts/Land-Verdict.ps1`), and verify it (`scripts/Verify-Verdict.ps1`).

**Why:** on 2026-07-22 I began hand-copying a review verdict about my own design into
`docs/reviews/`. That is a hand-maintained mirror at a process boundary - the scar class
this repo carries - with the aggravating factor that the author being reviewed was doing
the copying. A softened phrase or a dropped finding would be undetectable. The owner cut
straight to the class: *"the fact we're not doing this is the class."* Verbatim-by-
construction beats verbatim-by-discipline.

The owner also corrected my diagnosis: Entire.io already provides durability (every
session checkpoints to a public branch). The actual gap was **addressability** - an
artifact buried in a multi-megabyte JSONL is safe and unusable. Persisted-but-unfindable
does not satisfy a promise to publish.

**How to apply:**
- Land every gate verdict as it happens; do not batch to session end. See
  [[owner-sleeps-during-long-runs]].
- Check Entire (`entire` CLI, `entire/checkpoints/v1` branch) before assuming anything is
  lost. I panicked about data loss that had not occurred because I did not check the tool
  that had been reporting itself all session.
- Fault-inject any guard before trusting it. All three defects in this harness were
  fidelity bugs that would have made the word VERBATIM quietly false, and the worst one
  (a separator that also occurs in the payload) was found ONLY by tampering with a file
  and watching the checker fail to notice.

content.text:
Memory written — and I've noted that silence is sleep, not consent, so I won't read an unanswered question as approval.

**Where things stand:** rev 3 of the yard design is committed and *unreviewed* — the design gate is open and I'm parking it deliberately, not forgetting it.

Now, on the harness. Before I design anything, here's the reframe I think you may be pointing at, because it changes what the harness *is*:

**My solution is archaeology.** It recovers artifacts after the fact by parsing a vendor's private transcript format. Everything wrong with it descends from that one choice — the coupling, the hand-typed metadata, the fragility, the fact that I'm reconstructing something that was already structured and then thrown away.

**A keystone version produces the artifact as a first-class output at dispatch time.** The brief tells the agent to end inside a delimited envelope we define, with structured front-matter. Then landing parses *our* contract, not Anthropic's internals; the verdict, severity counts, and base SHA come from the reviewer rather than from me typing them; and a future runner changing its transcript format breaks nothing, because the envelope is ours.

And there's a consequence that I think makes this genuinely a keystone rather than tooling:

**The harness's output is StarCar's data source.** Gate verdicts, REJECT rounds, car reports, honest stops, dispatch cost — that *is* the freight the board renders. Right now the design assumes a conductor hand-writes `state.json`. But if every dispatch emits a structured artifact, the yard state can be **derived from the artifacts the process already produces**, which kills the hand-maintained state file that D3 and D5 exist to prop up. The board would stop being something the conductor maintains and start being something the process *emits*.

That would be a real Law 6 improvement — adapters own the facts, and the facts would come from the work itself rather than from someone remembering to write them down. It's also the same class we just closed, one level up.

Which raises the fork I can't call for you:

questions.question: What IS this harness? The answer changes the design more than any technical choice, because it decides whether the yard's state file survives at all.

options.label: Core product - dispatch artifacts become the board's data (Recommended)
options.description: Every dispatch emits a structured artifact; the yard adapter reads those artifacts. Verdicts, REJECT rounds, car reports and costs stop being hand-written into state.json and start being emitted by the work itself. Law 6 properly honoured - the facts come from the source that produced them. It also means the harness ships to strangers under Law 7, and it substantially rewrites the v0 design I just spent three revisions on.
options.preview:
dispatch -> structured artifact -> adapter -> board

+ state.json stops being hand-maintained
+ Law 6: facts come from the work itself
+ the showcase renders its OWN process, for real
-  v0 design gets rewritten AGAIN
-  harness must ship, Law 7 applies

options.label: Process tooling - keystone, but not shipped product
options.description: The harness is how THIS shop runs trains, held to the ladder because everything depends on it, but not part of what a stranger deploys. The yard adapter keeps reading a conductor-maintained state file; artifacts are for the record and the showcase, not for the board. Smaller, faster, and leaves the v0 design standing as revised.
options.preview:
dispatch -> artifact -> docs/reviews/ (the record)
conductor -> state.json -> adapter -> board

+ v0 design survives rev 3 intact
+ smaller scope, faster to land
-  state.json stays hand-maintained
-  two sources of the same truth

options.label: Both, staged - tooling now, data source later
options.description: Design the artifact contract ONCE and properly, so it can feed the board later, but ship only the record-keeping half now. The v0 yard design stands; a future train swaps its adapter from the state file to the artifact store. Costs a deliberate seam we must not get wrong, and risks the classic never-arrives-later.
options.preview:
now:   dispatch -> artifact -> the record
later: artifact store -> adapter -> board

+ contract designed once, correctly
+ v0 ships without another rewrite
-  a seam we must design blind
-  'later' has a way of never arriving

content.text:
That's the bigger call and it reshapes the yard design. Three consequences I'll derive now rather than ask about, because I think they follow necessarily:

**1. `state.json` doesn't die — it shrinks to intent.** Most yard facts *will* come from artifacts (a dispatch starting, a car reporting, a verdict landing, cost burned). But some facts no artifact can know: which tickets compose a train, and a dispatcher's hold under Law 2. So the split becomes **the conductor declares intent; the process emits facts.** That's cleaner than rev 3's two-fact-domain seam, and it satisfies Law 6 properly — the two sources own genuinely different things rather than racing on the same field.

**2. The artifact schema is the product; extractors are adapters.** Law 7 says a stranger's shop runs a different agent runner, so the shipped thing is a documented artifact format anyone can emit. `Land-Verdict.ps1` demotes from "the harness" to "the Claude Code producer" — one implementation of a contract, exactly like `StateFileAdapter` is one implementation of `Adapter<T>`.

**3. It's not just verdicts.** A dispatch start, a car report, an honest stop, a REJECT round, and a cost line are all yard events. The contract has to cover the dispatch lifecycle, not the review verdict specially.

And that surfaces a spend question I'd rather ask than assume, since it's real money:

questions.question: Rev 3 of the yard design is committed and unreviewed, and the decision you just made will substantially rewrite it - the state file shrinks to intent, and a whole adapter changes. Do we still spend a dispatch reviewing it?

options.label: Park rev 3 unreviewed; harness design first (Recommended)
options.description: Reviewing a document we already know is about to be rewritten spends an Opus dispatch on a moving target, and its findings would be against contracts that are changing. Park it with an honest status line saying WHY it is unreviewed, design the harness through the ladder, then revise the yard design against the harness contract and review it once, properly. Saves a dispatch and reviews the right artifact.
options.preview:
now:    harness design -> review -> spec/plan
then:   yard design rev 4, against the
        harness contract -> review ONCE

+ no dispatch spent on a moving target
+ reviews the artifact that will survive
-  rev 3's specific defects stay unfound
   (some may be inherited into rev 4)

options.label: Review rev 3 anyway before rewriting
options.description: Its composition section, register derivation, error table and test strategy are largely independent of where the data comes from, so a review would still catch real defects that would otherwise be inherited straight into rev 4. Costs one dispatch on a document that will change underneath the findings.
options.preview:
rev 3 -> review (findings banked)
then   -> rewrite as rev 4 carrying them

+ catches inheritable defects NOW
+ composition/tests are source-agnostic
-  one dispatch on a moving target
-  some findings will be moot by rev 4

options.label: Roll the harness into the yard design as one document
options.description: Stop treating them as two designs. One document covering artifact contract, producers, adapters and board, reviewed once as a whole. Fewer gates and no seam between two designs that must agree. Cost: a much larger document, and our own review found that rev 2's biggest failure was a contract whose pieces were specified separately and never composed.
options.preview:
ONE design: artifacts + adapters + board
  -> one adversarial review

+ no seam between two designs
+ fewer gates, less ceremony
-  large document, harder to review well
-  rev 2's lesson: big contracts fail at
   the composition, and this adds one

cd /c/Users/Chris/git/starcar
gh issue create --repo polecatspeaks/StarCar --label "area:adapters" \
  --title "The dispatch harness: every dispatch emits a structured artifact, and the board reads them" \
  --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
Owner ruling, 2026-07-22: **the dispatch harness is core product, not process tooling.** Every dispatch emits a structured artifact; the yard adapter reads those artifacts. This supersedes parts of the v0 yard design (now parked at rev 3, deliberately unreviewed - see the status block at the top of `docs/design/2026-07-21-v0-yard-skeleton-design.md`).

## Why this is a keystone

Gate verdicts, REJECT rounds, car reports, honest stops and cost lines ARE the freight the board renders. Rev 3 assumed a conductor hand-writes `state.json`. If the process emits its own facts instead, the board stops being something the conductor maintains and becomes something the work itself produces - which is Law 6 honoured properly, with facts coming from the source that generated them.

## Three consequences already derived

1. **`state.json` shrinks to INTENT.** Some facts no artifact can know: which tickets compose a train, and a dispatcher's hold under Law 2. So the conductor declares intent; the process emits facts. Two sources owning genuinely different things, rather than rev 3's two-fact-domain seam where both could touch `position`.
2. **The artifact schema is the product; extractors are adapters.** Law 7: a stranger's shop runs a different agent runner, so the shipped thing is a documented format anyone can emit. `scripts/Land-Verdict.ps1` demotes from "the harness" to "the Claude Code producer" - one implementation of a contract, exactly as `StateFileAdapter` is one implementation of `Adapter<T>`.
3. **It is not just verdicts.** Dispatch start, car report, honest stop, REJECT round, cost - the contract covers the dispatch lifecycle, not the review verdict specially.

## What exists today, and why it is only a start

`scripts/Land-Verdict.ps1` + `scripts/Verify-Verdict.ps1` (landed in `8787d9a`) extract a verdict verbatim from the session transcript and hash it so the verbatim claim is checkable. Both round-1 and round-2 verdicts are landed and verified.

Six known limits, recorded by the author before any review:

1. **Vigilance wearing a script's clothing** - the conductor must still remember to run it, with seven hand-typed parameters. The class was "artifacts survive only if someone remembers"; the remembering moved, it did not go away.
2. **`-Verdict` is hand-typed by the author being reviewed** - the body is verbatim, its summary is an assertion. The same mirror class, one layer up.
3. **Hardcoded project path** in the transcript lookup. A Law 7 violation: a stranger cannot run it.
4. **Couples to Claude Code's private transcript format** (`content[].text`, `<task-id>`, `<result>`) - undocumented internals in another vendor's repo, with no contract.
5. **No index.** Ten dispatches per train times N trains and `docs/reviews/` becomes the same unnavigable pile - persisted-but-unfindable, the exact class this was meant to close.
6. **The harness has had zero adversarial review**, and produced three fidelity bugs on first contact (ANSI decoding mangling non-ASCII; a BOM/CRLF write that made every file fail its own verifier; a separator that also occurs in the payload, which made the checker structurally unable to detect a REJECT flipped to APPROVE while crying wolf on good files). The harness that enforces the process was exempt from the process. **This issue closes that exemption.**

Defect 6.3 was found ONLY by fault injection - tampering with a landed file and watching the checker fail to notice. The repo's rule that a guard is unproven until someone has watched it fire, applied to the guard that enforces the rules.

## Route

Full ladder: design -> adversarial design review -> spec -> spec review -> plan -> plan review -> cars. Then `docs/design/2026-07-21-v0-yard-skeleton-design.md` returns as rev 4, written against this contract, and is reviewed once.

Labelled `area:adapters` because the artifact store is a data source and the contract is adapter-facing; the producers are tooling, but the product half dominates. Flagged rather than assumed.
EOF
)"
echo "--- filed ---"
input.description: File the harness design issue

--- filed ---
